method: ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer API v2 for EIN resolution and filing lists; IRS e-file XML fetched via projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/download-xml?object_id=...; Part VII Section B parsed from ContractorCompensationGrp / CntrctRcvdGreaterThan100KCnt. Latest two XML-available filings per EIN. Public filings; entries recorded verbatim; no inference beyond the filing text.
transport_keyword_regex: ambulanc|medical transport|patient transport|transport(?:ation)? of patients|\bems\b|emergency medical|air medical|life ?flight|med ?flight|medevac|medivac|paramedic|ambulette|american medical response|\bamr\b|ambulnz|\bnemt\b|critical care transport|mobile intensive care|superior air|midwest medical transport|transport

systems.disambiguation_notes: DISAMBIGUATION RULE: study footprint is Midwest/Ohio Valley. Among US 'Baptist Health' systems (Little Rock AR 71-0236856; Montgomery AL 63-0366289; Jacksonville FL 59-2487136; Pensacola FL 59-2425151; Birmingham AL 63-0312913; South Florida; Baptist Memorial Memphis TN), only Baptist Health Kentucky (legal name Baptist Healthcare System Inc, Louisville KY, EIN 61-0444707) operates hospitals in the Ohio Valley: nine KY hospitals plus Baptist Health Floyd in New Albany, Indiana, per its public system profile. Selected on that basis. A second Louisville entity with the same name (61-6145328, ~$6.8M revenue, latest filing 2021) is a small affiliate and was excluded.

headline_result: Across 46 parsed filings (latest two XML-available Form 990s per resolved EIN), zero of the disclosed five-highest-compensated independent contractors per filing had a name or services description matching ambulance / EMS / medical transport keywords. Form 990 Part VII Section B discloses only the top five contractors over $100,000, so transport vendors billing below each filer's fifth-highest contractor would not appear; the count of contractors over $100,000 (contractors_gt100k_count) is recorded per filing as the information floor.
